Applications Open for Summer 2027 Fellows

July 1, 2026 · darshanaswathappa

Applications for the Summer 2027 WordPress research fellowship application are now open. Every year, the Northeastern University Department of Software Engineering invites a cohort of undergraduate students to join a ten-week paid research program. This year, we are expanding to six research tracks and welcoming a larger, more diverse group of fellows than ever before.

What the WordPress Research Fellowship Application 2027 Covers

The application asks for three things: a short statement of interest, a code sample or portfolio link, and a brief description of the problem in WordPress you most want to solve. You do not need prior WordPress experience. Strong PHP or JavaScript fundamentals are enough to get started. In fact, many of our most successful fellows arrived knowing only one of those languages.

Furthermore, we look for curiosity and follow-through over specific tool experience. If you have shipped something small — a plugin, a theme, a CLI tool, a browser extension — that tells us more than a transcript. We want to see that you can take an idea from nothing to something working.

New Research Tracks for 2027

In addition to the existing tracks in plugin architecture, editor UX, performance, and accessibility, we are adding two new areas this year:

  • Developer Tooling — CLI tools, code generation, and local development workflows.
  • Cloud and Deployment — containerisation, multisite orchestration, and edge delivery for WordPress.

Each track is led by a faculty mentor and supported by an industry partner from the WordPress ecosystem. Fellows meet weekly as a full cohort and also in smaller track teams. By the end of week ten, every track ships a working, publicly available deliverable.

Timeline and Stipend

The program runs from June 2 to August 8, 2027. Fellows receive a stipend of $6,000 for the ten weeks. Housing support is available for students relocating to Boston for the summer. The application deadline is December 1, 2026, and decisions go out by February 1, 2027.

Therefore, if you are planning to apply for this WordPress research fellowship, we strongly recommend starting your statement of interest now rather than waiting until late November. The strongest applications we receive every year come from students who have clearly thought about the specific problem they want to work on.

How to Apply

Visit the Apply and Eligibility page for the full requirements and the application form. If you have questions, reach out to the program faculty through the Contact page. We also hold two open question-and-answer sessions via Zoom in October and November — dates will be posted on this blog.

We look forward to reading your applications. Good luck.
